focus: Concrete validator+extraction fixes for the two known prose hallucinations (focus-consistent hard check; named-principle token-binding; claims-to-token provenance mechanism)
answeredQuestions: Can a 99/100 validator score be trivially gamed by prose-only hallucinations? (Yes — zero of 6 checks read sentences.)
Why does extractFocusIndicator fabricate consistent:true when no data exists? (Default-return bug at a11y-extract.ts:101-103, hit because interaction capture is OFF by default.)

What mechanical coupling exists between the format/style specs and the AI's incentive to invent named depth principles? (design_md_format.md §9 REQUIRES a named principle with custom-name escape hatch; writing_style_guide.md §16+§12 actively pattern-train the AI to produce technique-as-metaphor names.)

focus: Borrowable anti-fabrication architectures from the external landscape (coverage-based token election, DTCG provenance, evidence-gated narrative)
answeredQuestions: Which 3–5 techniques most directly prevent AI-prose-fabrication (ranked)? → Coverage-based election (#1), DTCG $type/$value schema (#2), thin-evidence omission (#3), deterministic section quotas (#4), explicit component mapping (#5)

Does coverage-based token election reduce L4 leakage better than OKLCH+L1-L4 gating? → Yes. Current gating uses additive scoring where high element-frequency can override low page-coverage. Coverage-based election makes page-level prevalence the primary gate, catching one-off page tokens that L1-L4 scoring misclassifies as L2 due to element count. The current pagesCoverage field contributes only 20-30 points out of a possible 60-100 — a token at 0% page coverage can still reach L2 if it has high frequency + CSS variable presence + high usage dimensions.

Does DTCG $type/$value give stronger provenance anchoring than current tokens.json? → Yes. Current tokens.json has flat arrays (colorTokens[], shadowTokens[], radiusTokens[]) with no typed cross-references. DTCG's mandatory $type prevents category errors, enables explicit token references ({ $value: '{color.brand.primary}' }), and makes 'gradient-as-depth' claims auditable because each $type has defined semantics. The current shape captures MORE raw data (frequency distributions, usage contexts) but has WEAKER structural provenance for prose claims.

Does any tool constrain or omit narrative when evidence is thin? → Yes. Superposition EXCLUDES unused styles entirely. CSS Stats clusters purely by occurrence with no interpretive overlay. Both demonstrate that omission is the correct anti-fabrication posture. sk-design-md-generator's current writing guide REQUIRES named principles (AP-25 treats unnamed principles as a defect) and encourages interpretive prose even with sparse data — the opposite approach.

focus: Extraction gaps that force invention (interaction OFF by default, a11y async half uncalled, single-page motion, contrast top-20 cap, gradient non-decomposition)
answeredQuestions: Which extraction gaps are the primary upstream causes of DESIGN.md hallucination (Track B hypothesis)
What is the exact mechanism by which missing interaction data propagates to §11/§4/§9 fabrication
Is the a11y async extraction dead code or intentionally deferred
Does the contrast-pair cap affect §9 (answer: no, §9 uses uncapped extractA11y pairs; the cap affects §2 colorRelationships only)

focus: Widen validator mechanical reach (stability-gating beyond colors; banned-word check; split values-vs-claims score; font allowlist)
answeredQuestions: Can checkStabilityGating be mechanically extended beyond colorTokens? Yes — ShadowToken/RadiusToken/ComponentGroup/TypographyLevel all carry stability fields; gradients need the field added.
Can banned-word discipline be enforced without false positives? Yes — sentence-level numeric-anchor detection suppresses warnings for value-bound prose while catching empty filler.
Can the single score be split without breaking existing consumers? Yes — ValidationResult gains valuesScore/claimsScore alongside the existing score field; isPass adds a claimsScore gate.

Is checkUnknownFonts' skip-list a real fabrication risk? Moderate — the explicit system-font allowlist masks legitimate 'font not in extraction' warnings, but the heuristic pre-filters catch most random fabrication.

focus: Remove fabrication incentives from format-spec/style-guide (conditional named-principle, AP-29, ABSENT-stamp escalation)
answeredQuestions: All mandatory philosophy/named-principle clauses in the format spec (Depth §9, Motion §10, Visual Theme §1) are unconditional — they demand narrative REGARDLESS of data presence, creating a systemic fabrication incentive.

SKILL.md's NEVER rules and cardinal fidelity contract cover ONLY quantitative values (hex, px, weight, shadow, radius). No clause prohibits asserting qualitative relationships, causes, or design intent not in tokens.json.

The writing_style_guide.md is the primary fabrication driver: always-loaded, and §15-§18 actively solicit interpretive claims (comparative framing, named principles, intent narration, frequency interpretation) beyond what CSS extraction can yield.
Style guide §10's 'deletion test' (paragraph with zero values = opinion) directly contradicts format spec's mandatory philosophy paragraphs. Both are always-loaded. The contradiction is unresolved.
A missing anti-pattern (AP-29) and a missing stamp-ABSENT/escalate path leave the model with no supported alternative to fabrication when data is absent.

focus: OKLCH clustering + L1-L4 stability gating + variant/component classification accuracy
answeredQuestions: B3: deltaE<3 is too tight — causes under-clustering, splitting brand colors, diluting frequency/pagesCoverage that drive stability classification
B4: The hue circular-distance formula is correct for all h∈[0,360]; the modulo is dead code but harmless; no failure case exists

B10: The frequency signal is underweighted (±20 of 120) relative to pagesCoverage/cssVar/usage, enabling L4 leakage to L2 via the path: pagesCoverage≥0.5 (+20) + cssVar (+20) + text/border (+25) + freq≤5 (−20) = 45 ≥ 30 → L2
B11: Default-to-Primary fallthrough masks Outline/Tertiary/Link variants; hard RGB Destructive heuristic false-positives on red-brand sites and misses non-red destructive colors
B12: Geometric thresholds (Badge height<30, Card childrenCount≥2, width 200-800, padding≥12) are tuned for one site density and will miss common variants across different information architectures

focus: Adopt coverage-based token election + DTCG $type/$value provenance from design-extract
answeredQuestions: Coverage election (binary site-wide share threshold) IS more robust against single-page one-off leakage than our composite scoring because it's a HARD GATE: a token appearing on 1 page never reaches 'system' regardless of frequency. Our 5-signal composite can be gamed on single-page extractions where pageCoverage=1.0. Slot it as a PRE-FILTER before classifyColorStability() in cluster.ts:569-843.

DTCG $type/$value IS a substantially stronger claims-to-token binding than hex-only sentinels. A hex check (validate.ts:57-93) treats all color usages identically regardless of semantic role. A typed token anchors every DESIGN.md claim to a specific $type slot — a 'gradient-depth' claim becomes unprovable if no $type:"gradient" token exists, enforced at validation time rather than relying on the writer's discipline.

Composite/semantic tokens give the writer a bounded, machine-verifiable vocabulary. Instead of free prose inventing concepts like 'layered floating cards,' the writer must cite `shadow.card.elevated` (a composite referencing primitive colors). This eliminates the largest source of unverifiable claims.

focus: Constrained 9-section schemas (DesignMD/Open Design) + clean-input filtering (Superposition/CSS Stats) vs fabrication
answeredQuestions: Which v2 sections are fabrication-prone philosophy sections that 9-section tools omit? Sections 0, 1, 6, 7, 6.5, and 3 (Named Strategies) — these mandate narrative prose even when data is thin.

Do 9-section tools require narrative at all? No — they are value-tables (palette, typography scale, spacing, components) with constraints (do/don't). Narrative is the wrong default for anti-hallucination docs.

Does pre-writer input filtering (unused-style exclusion, occurrence-clustering) reduce fabrication? Yes — it removes noise before the writer sees it, preventing one-off values from being elevated to 'design principles'.
What is the single most adoptable schema change? Convert Sections 0, 1, 6, 7 from mandatory-narrative to table-first with optional prose gated by data-density thresholds.

focus: Remaining structural fixes (comparative-framing removal, structured-output mode, adversarial self-review, default-fabrication audit)
answeredQuestions: A8: Yes, comparative framing (format §3.4 + style §15) is a built-in structural fabrication mandate — it REQUIRES claims about other design systems that tokens.json cannot verify. Must be removed or constrained to intra-system comparisons.

A11: Three highest-risk prose sections (Depth philosophy, Motion philosophy, Focus consistency) should be replaced with token-bound templated fields (value | token-key | optional 1-line note). Field shapes defined per section above.

A15: Post-write adversarial self-review pass designed: sentence-level anchoring audit (ANCHORED vs INTERPRETIVE) slotted between WRITE and VALIDATE. Any INTERPRETIVE sentence must be anchored or deleted before validate.ts runs.

A-i: The bug pattern IS replicated — minTouchTarget (0/0 on empty), altTextCoverage (100% on empty), extractFocusIndicator ({},true on empty) all fabricate defaults indistinguishable from real data. Uniform absent→null+captured:false convention proposed.

focus: Token-transform frameworks (Style Dictionary/Theo/Terrazzo/Diez/DTCG) — typed tokens + deterministic value-table generation
answeredQuestions: DTCG $type/$value adoption: what concretely improves (provenance, validator binding, emit interop) and what breaks (cluster.ts wrapper, validate.ts type-gating, types.ts expansion)
Deterministic value-tables from typed tokens: eliminates typography/shadow/component fabrication that checkPhantomColors misses — 6 DESIGN.md sections become deterministic, 10 stay AI-written
Transform/format separation: cleaner pipeline where deterministic formatters own value-tables and AI owns narrative — no Style Dictionary dependency needed

focus: Feed visual/structural sections (gradient decomposition, breakpoint key-changes, section coverage report)
answeredQuestions: B6: Is gradient decomposition possible from raw strings? Yes — the raw linear-gradient(...) string contains angle and color-stop data that a regex parser can extract. Current code only regex-extracts colors (extractGradientColors in cluster.ts:244-261), leaving angle and stop positions unused.

B8: Can extraction diff computed layout across viewports to populate breakpoint keyChanges? Architecturally feasible (Playwright already resizes for screenshots at 5 widths), but single-viewport DOM collection (extract.ts:298) means no baseline exists for diffing. Multi-viewport re-extraction or explicit ABSENT flag needed.

B15: Does any existing tool produce a token→section coverage map? No — report-gen, preview-gen, proof, and validate each serve different purposes (HTML dashboard, token preview, fidelity proof, hex/section-header validation) and none maps DESIGN.md sections to their backing token set cardinality.

focus: Static CSS analysis (project-wallace/CSS Stats/Parker) — dedup, occurrence clustering, hybrid static+computed
answeredQuestions: How rigorous is project-wallace's dedup/normalization compared to our extraction, and would adopting its approach reduce noise that inflates our token set and leaks one-offs?
How does CSS Stats' occurrence-based color clustering compare to our OKLCH perceptual clustering for deciding system vs one-off colors, and can they be combined?

What is the static-parse vs computed-style trade-off, and is there a hybrid that gets both CSS-custom-property declarations (static, names) AND computed values (runtime) for var-name + resolved-value provenance?
How does get-custom-properties' structured extraction compare to our flat concat of cssVariables?

focus: Measured a11y + typography fidelity (CIEDE2000 contrast over all pairs, nearest-compliant shade, OpenType font metrics, axe-style audit)
answeredQuestions: Q1: Yes — §9 should use a11y-extract.ts real DOM pairs, not cluster.ts top-20 synthetic cross-product. Source: a11y-extract.ts:265-304.
Q2: Yes — nearest-compliant-shade via OKLCh binary search is feasible with existing culori/deltaE. Gate as RECOMMENDED by verifying suggested hex is absent from tokens.colorTokens.
Q3: Feasible via opentype.js parsing .woff2 font URLs already captured by dom-collector.ts. x-height/cap-height are highest-value metrics. High effort — defer to iteration 15.
Q4: Yes — extractFocusIndicator() default-true at line 102 is the specific fabrication. Replace with page.evaluate() counting interactive elements without visible focus styles.

focus: Component/state fidelity from design-to-code tools (semantic mapping over geometry, evidence-gated variants, §11 hard-gate to interaction capture)
answeredQuestions: Q1: Semantic component signals ARE already collected (className, role, ariaLabel, nearestLandmark in dom-collector.ts:193/194/195/263/262) but cluster.ts detection at line 1134 ignores them entirely — the borrowable signal is class-name pattern matching + ARIA role before geometric fallback, not instead of it

Q2: classifyVariant at cluster.ts:1256 needs an evidence-gate — the fallthrough `return 'Primary'` should become `return 'unclassified'` so the writer doesn't treat all unclassified elements as a named variant with inflated counts

Q3: §11 hard-gate requires three changes: flip default to --with-interaction in extract.ts:81, add null-state rendering rule to format spec, add fabrication detection to validate.ts — all three are needed because the current system incentivizes invention at every layer

Q4: Anima's Auto Layout mapping IS borrowable because dom-collector.ts already collects flex/grid properties — they just aren't aggregated into structural tokens in cluster.ts:1368. Adding a 15-line aggregation block turns collected-but-unused CSS into §5-evidenced layout tokens

focus: Remaining context levers (observed/recommended boundary, cardinal-rules pre-write gate, AP-29 at VALIDATE, fidelity-audit intent, sentinels, ABSENT-stamp)
answeredQuestions: Where does the observed-vs-recommended boundary blur? design_md_format.md §10 Motion fallback prose—the sole template-level source of fabricated design advice.
Should cardinal_rules_card.md constrain generation? Yes—it must be added to ALWAYS resource load level and injected into the prompt template as a pre-write gate, not kept as post-hoc audit.
Is anti_patterns.md actually absent at VALIDATE? No—the SKILL.md §2 table includes it at line 105. The real gap is validate.ts being structurally blind to all 28 anti-patterns.

What distinguishes fidelity-audit from VALIDATE? Fidelity-audit checks prose claims against observed evidence; VALIDATE checks structural conformance (hex accuracy, section presence). They are complementary passes.
Inline sentinels vs claims.json sidecar? Inline sentinels win on portability, survival through copy-paste, and no desync risk. stripHtmlComments already handles stripping.

focus: Token storage/provenance disciplines (typed collections+manifest, aliasing/references, composite tokens)
answeredQuestions: Would splitting into typed collections + manifest let a writer cite specific token slots? Yes — the manifest gives stable file+key addresses (e.g. color-tokens.json#idx-3) vs. scanning a 4k-line array.

Does aliasing give a safer role vocabulary than OKLCH heuristics? Yes — cssVariableNames already carry the site author's semantic intent (--color-text-primary); aliasing captures this as provenance rather than discarding it after cross-referencing hexes.

Does compositing reduce the writer's reassembly step? Yes — structured shadow objects eliminate CSS-string parsing; typography composites eliminate the fontFamily+size+weight reassembly; both close the fabrication gap.

focus: Grounded AI documentation (doc-as-view-over-tokens, drafting guardrails, anti-stale regenerate-tables, MCP token endpoint)
answeredQuestions: Doc = a view over tokens is the correct model — 5 of 17 DESIGN.md sections (Colors, Typography, Shadows, Radii, Spacing) should be deterministic renders from tokens.json, not AI prose
Zeroheight constrains AI by coupling drafts to synced source data + human refinement — borrowable as cite-the-token + draft-then-verify
Anti-stale merge model: auto-regenerate value-tables, preserve delimited human annotations, flag stale AI sections — implementable as merge-update.ts
MCP token endpoint is relevant but lower priority — agents already read tokens.json; the real win is fixing the WRITE phase split

focus: DESIGN.md schema peers (Open Design 9-section incl anti-patterns/decisions/references; spec-honesty 17->9-10 sections; skill grounding pattern)
answeredQuestions: Q1: Open Design's ANTI-PATTERNS and DECISIONS sections are higher-signal than our philosophy-heavy sections because they require token evidence per entry (a DECISION without cited tokens can't exist; an ANTI-PATTERN without a specific violated rule is meaningless). Our Sections 1 (Atmosphere) and 7 (Content & Voice) invite fabrication because they require LLM-invented concepts (metaphors, vibe) with no token-evidence quota. ANTI-PATTERNS should be promoted from our internal anti_patterns.md resource doc INTO the DESIGN.md output as a new section; DECISIONS is a missing section entirely that would replace scattered-in-prose rationale with auditable provenance.

Q2: Of our 8 extra sections beyond Open Design's 9, the value-adding ones are: Dark Mode (2.5, conditional/data-driven), Accessibility Contract (9, computed ratios), State Matrix (11, extractable states), Agent Prompt Guide (13, self-contained utility). The fabrication-inviting ones are: Brand Context (0, personality adjectives from LLM), Visual Theme & Atmosphere (1, 200-300 words of metaphors), Content & Voice (7, vibe paragraph). Recommended revision: demote 0, 7, 11, 12 to CONDITIONAL (only when tokens contain sufficient data); add DECISIONS section with token-evidence requirement; promote ANTI-PATTERNS into output. Net section count: 13 core + 5 conditional = honest 13-18 output, not claimed 17.

Q3: extract-design-system's prompt-driven architecture eliminates the interpretive WRITE phase entirely — the skill prompt IS the extraction, outputting tokens.json + tokens.css with zero AI-prose. Dembrandt is pure CLI, similarly avoiding AI prose. Our hybrid architecture (CLI extract → AI write → CLI validate) keeps the AI-prose phase as the value proposition but should adopt their grounding pattern: front-load token constraints in the WRITE prompt so EVERY prose claim must cite token evidence (adoptable via prompt template change in SKILL.md). Dembrandt's static+computed hybrid would improve token completeness (capturing unused CSS custom properties).

Q4: YES — multi-agent consumption strongly argues for MORE structured output. Our Section 13 (Agent Prompt Guide) already proves this: self-contained prompts work identically for any agent. The prose sections (1, 7) produce divergent interpretations across agents. The fix is a dual-layer document: structured key-value summary at the top of each prose section (machine-consumable) + freeform prose below (human context). Open Design's deterministic re-render achieves this by constraining section content types — we should add structured summaries to Sections 1, 3, 5, 6.

focus: Sampling precision + per-token provenance (element-level capture, where-used anchor, confidence marking, value-only export)
answeredQuestions: Q1: Element-level provenance — yes, the data is captured in dom-collector.ts but lost at cluster.ts clustering; adding selectorAnchors to ColorToken is high-value and straightforward.
Q2: Confidence/provenance gating — confidence field exists but is not a promotion gate; coupling it to stability classification prevents low-evidence token promotion.
Q3: Value-only export — tokens.json already contains all values; a curated .values.json mirroring DESIGN.md structure without prose is a clean additional output mode.
Q4: Color precision gaps — gradient CSS-string parsing misses interpolated colors; opacity compositing is not performed; canvas-based sampling would close both gaps.

focus: Remaining correctness fixes (findInteraction matching, classifyShadow dedup, touch-target filtering, extractA11yAsync wiring)
answeredQuestions: Confirmed extractA11yAsync is entirely dead code — never imported or called, so tabOrder/lang/skipLink/altText never populate the output

Confirmed classifyShadow duplication: two identical copies in cluster.ts at lines 374 and 1047, the inline one is authoritative for shadowTokens production, the exported one is dead for production use

Confirmed findInteraction drops real interaction data for <a> tags and role-based components due to componentType-vs-tag key mismatch; the linear-scan fallback has a class-substring direction that fails when the representative has fewer classes than the captured element

focus: Fabrication-safe reverse-engineering methodology (deterministic inference, functional naming, value-tables-over-prose)
answeredQuestions: Step 4 (system inference — L1-L4) is DETERMINISTIC in our pipeline, unlike LLM-based methodologies. The defensible inference rule is: CSS variable naming (strongest) > cross-page consistency > within-site percentile frequency > usage-dimension diversity > chromaticity. Our pipeline uses all except cross-page consistency (computed but not scored) and percentile ranking (uses absolute thresholds).

Step 5 (role naming) — the anti-fabrication discipline from published methodologies IS functional/positional naming, and our color_role_taxonomy.md already defines 19 functional roles with deterministic identification signals. The gap: cluster.ts doesn't execute these signals algorithmically — it defers to the LLM. Closing this gap is the single highest-value anti-fabrication move.

Narrative is appropriate ONLY in §0 Brand Context and §1 Visual Theme & Atmosphere. The consensus is value-tables + factual annotations for the token catalog. Our current format spec mandates narrative in ALL sections, which is the OPPOSITE of the fabrication-safe consensus.

focus: Anti-fabrication pattern taxonomy + DTCG spine (ranked value/cost; minimal viable adoption set)
answeredQuestions: DTCG as spine: tokens.json→DTCG migration enables (b) structurally and anchors (f). DTCG $type authority proves provenance without procedural trust. Composite tokens with aliasing create auditable token graphs.

DESIGN.md claim→DTCG slot mapping: §2/§2.5→color; §3→fontFamily+fontWeight+dimension; §4→color+dimension+shadow+transition; §5→dimension; §6→shadow; §6.5→duration+cubicBezier; §11→color+shadow+transition; §12→dimension+number. Prose sections (§0/§1/§7/§8) cite token $description as claim-anchor.

Top 3-4 patterns to reduce prose-fabrication: (b)+(f)+(h)+(c). They compose tightly — DTCG types tokens (b) → coverage classifies them (c) → functional naming labels them (h) → deterministic rendering surfaces them as tables + $description citations (f). The composition eliminates the LLM as a value-invention surface.

Minimal viable adoption: DTCG-typed tokens.json + $description field on every token + prose sections cite $description. Full ideal adds CIEDE2000, composite token graphs, and fully programmatic DESIGN.md generation.

focus: Adversarial verification of top P0 recommendations
answeredQuestions: Does the focus-consistent bug root-cause line still exist? YES — a11y-extract.ts:101-103 returns consistent:true on empty interactions, interaction capture is OFF by default (extract.ts:81), and validate.ts never checks the Accessibility Contract prose against tokens.

Would removing mandatory philosophy lose genuine value? YES — evidence-backed named principles (e.g., 'shadow-as-border' with real shadow frequency data) are the highest-value prose in a design doc. The fix is evidence-gating, not removal.

Does DTCG adoption directly fix prose fabrication? NO — token typing addresses structural provenance, but prose claims like 'gradient creates atmospheric depth' are a prompt/writer discipline issue, not a type system issue. DTCG has high interop value but should not block anti-fabrication fixes.

Are 5 required sections never written in practice? YES — validated across 4 gold-standard examples (Vercel 10 sections, Stripe 9, Linear 9, Supabase ~9). The validator enforces 15 section headers but ~5 have zero backing token data in typical extractions.

Would coverage-based election regress current behavior? NO — it would CORRECT misclassifications where single-page tokens leak into L2 via high element-frequency overriding low page-coverage. It should be additive (pre-gate), not replacement.

focus: Agent-consumption format (structured tokens primary, tokens.css output, MCP endpoint, DESIGN.md as rendered view)
answeredQuestions: Is prose-heavy DESIGN.md the wrong primary artifact for AI agent consumption? → YES — tokens.json should be primary; DESIGN.md a deterministic render + AI annotation overlay. (iter-011, iter-017, iter-020)

Should sk-design-md-generator emit tokens.css? → YES — trivial transform from tokens.json, directly importable by agents, proven in anobel.com production. (extract-design-system pattern, anobel/004-bento-visuals)

Would an MCP token endpoint eliminate the prose-trust problem? → Partially — it eliminates numeric lookup trust but is lower priority than fixing the WRITE phase to be deterministic for value sections. CLI query-token.ts gives 80% of value. (iter-017 P2)

What borrowable patterns exist from extract-design-system's skill architecture? → Prompt-driven grounding: front-load token constraints so every prose claim cites evidence; never let the LLM free-write numeric values. (iter-018 P1)

focus: Confidence/grading as anti-fabrication signal (per-section evidence grade, per-token coverage confidence, measured a11y grade)
answeredQuestions: Q1: Should each DESIGN.md section carry a confidence grade? YES — the token-count-per-section data already exists (extraction pipeline computes it). A per-section report card is derivable: §6 Depth has shadowTokens.length/gradient count → confidence; §7 Content has NO token backing → EVIDENCE_ABSENT. Compute in cluster.ts post-token-assembly or in report-gen.ts pre-write.

Q2: Per-token confidence from coverage election already EXISTS in schema (ColorToken.confidence, TypographyLevel.confidence as 'high'|'medium'|'low') computed in cluster.ts:752,911. Schema: types.ts:424,439. The field is populated but not surfaced to the DESIGN.md writer — the writer should gate strong claims on high-confidence tokens only.

Q3: Should §9 carry a measured a11y grade? YES — the data exists (contrast ratios with AA/AAA booleans, focus consistency, touch targets) but no aggregate grade is computed. An aggregate is derivable and transcribable (not inventable). Compute in a11y-extract.ts or at write time from tokens.json a11yTokens.

Q4: Does graded output change the validator? The validator already IS graded (0-100 score with critical gating) — not purely binary. The next step is per-section evidence-completeness: flag sections that make claims with zero backing data as FABRICATED, sections with thin data as PARTIAL, sections with full token backing as VERIFIED. This upgrades the validator from format-check to evidence-audit.

focus: Track-A consolidated recommendation set (tiered, verification-applied)
answeredQuestions: Can a 99/100 validator score be trivially gamed by prose-only hallucinations? (Yes — zero of 6 checks read sentences)
Does the focus-consistent bug root-cause line still exist? (Yes — a11y-extract.ts:101-103 unchanged)
Would removing mandatory philosophy lose genuine value? (Yes — evidence-gate instead)
Does DTCG adoption directly fix prose fabrication? (No — prompt discipline issue, not type system)
Are 5 required sections never written in practice? (Yes — 9-10 in gold-standard examples)
Is the comparative-framing mandate a structural fabrication driver? (Yes — format+style REQUIRE unverifiable external claims)

focus: Track-B consolidated fix set (tiered by fabrication impact)
answeredQuestions: Which extraction gaps are primary upstream causes of DESIGN.md fabrication (Track-B core hypothesis): interaction OFF-by-default starves §11/§4/§9; extractA11yAsync dead code starves 5 §9 fields; deltaE under-clustering starves §2 brand colors; no coverage map makes ALL starvation invisible

Exact mechanism: interaction data propagation chain broken at two points — default OFF gates captureInteractions entirely; when ON, findInteraction component-type key mismatch drops data for <a> and role-based components
Is extractA11yAsync dead code or intentionally deferred: confirmed dead — exported but never imported, Page object closed (extract.ts:422) before any async a11y call could run
Does contrast cap affect §9: no — §9 a11y contrast uses uncapped extractA11y DOM pairs; cap on cluster.ts:799 affects §2 colorRelationships only
Is deltaE<3 correct: no — ~10× too tight for perceptual grouping, greedy single-pass with no recentering amplifies the split
Is hue circular-distance formula correct: yes — refuted B4, formula at cluster.ts:781-783 correctly computes circular distance for all h∈[0,360]
Which sections have zero backing tokens: confirmed §0/§1/§7/§8 have NO corresponding token fields in DesignTokens; §10 collapsing and §12 icon-alignment also architecturally unbacked

focus: Track-C consolidated change set (tiered doc/prompt/routing)
answeredQuestions: Which format-spec clauses unconditionally demand philosophy? §§1, 6 (Depth), 6.5 (Motion) — all three require named principles, philosophy paragraphs, or comparative framing regardless of token data presence (iter-006 C3, confirmed by iter-024 as SCOPED: evidence-gate, don't abolish).

Is the style-guide §10 deletion test contradicted by the format spec's mandatory philosophy? YES — the style guide says zero-value paragraphs are 'opinion' to be deleted; the format spec demands paragraphs that routinely contain zero token values. Both are ALWAYS-loaded. Resolution: amend style guide to accept evidence-backed claims; amend format spec to require evidence (iter-006 C11).

Does cutting 17 sections to ~9 core meaningfully reduce fabrication pressure? YES — iter-024 confirmed REAL: all 4 gold-standard examples produce 9-10 sections; the validator enforces 15 headers but 5 have zero backing token data, forcing fabrication. Demoting 0/7/11/12 to conditional eliminates header-enforced fabrication pressure.

Is the focus-consistent bug a Track-C concern? NO — it is an extract/code bug at a11y-extract.ts:101-103 (returns consistent:true on empty interactions). Confirmed REAL by iter-024 but outside Track-C doc/prompt/routing scope.

Should named principles be abolished entirely? NO — iter-024 explicitly scoped this: evidence-backed principles (e.g., 'shadow-as-border' with real frequency data) are the highest-value prose in a design doc. The fix is evidence-gating, not removal.

focus: Track-D ranked borrow list (ADOPT-NOW/SCOPED/DEFER, top-3 composable)
answeredQuestions: What are the ranked borrowable anti-fabrication techniques from ~20 external tools, deduplicated across 15 Track-D iterations?
How do iteration-24 adversarial verdicts (DTCG=SCOPED high-cost, section-cut=REAL, election=SCOPED-additive, named-principle=SCOPED) affect tier assignment?
Do the top 3 borrows (data-driven sections, coverage election, deterministic tables) compose into one coherent architecture?
Which techniques are already partially implemented (confidence field, stability gating, proof.ts) and only need plumbing?

focus: Doc-as-view-over-tokens target architecture (deterministic/grounded-annotation/bounded-prose section classes + deterministic renderer)
answeredQuestions: How to partition the 17 sections into three fabrication-risk classes: Class (a) DETERMINISTIC = §2,§2.5,§3,§5(spacing+radii),§6(shadow table),§6.5(duration+easing),§9(contrast),§10(breakpoints),§12(sizing),§17 (sections where every field has a tokens.json row and zero AI judgment is needed). Class (b) GROUNDED-ANNOTATION = §4,§5(whitespace philosophy),§6(named principle+contrast para),§6.5(motion philosophy+choreography),§8,§11 (sections where AI writes 1-line annotations that must cite >=1 token value each). Class (c) BOUNDED-PROSE = §0,§1,§7,§13(prompts),§14-16 (AI prose labeled [INFERRED], each paragraph citing >=2 token values).

Where the deterministic renderer lives and what it replaces: tool/scripts/formatters.ts — a new TypeScript script that reads tokens.json (post-cluster, post-DTCG-wrap) and emits markdown tables for all class-(a) sections. It runs BEFORE the AI WRITE phase as a pre-generation step. The AI receives the pre-rendered tables as input and is forbidden from modifying them — it writes only class-(b) annotation lines and class-(c) prose. This replaces the current WRITE phase where the AI generates ALL 17 sections including value tables.

How the token-citation requirement is enforced: validate.ts gains a new checkCitationGating() function that parses citation markers (format: `[token: <json-path>, <src-section>]`) in DESIGN.md prose and verifies each cited path exists in tokens.json. Class-(c) sections require >=2 citations per paragraph; class-(b) sections require >=1 citations per annotation line. Uncited claims in these sections are hard FAILURES. This composes with existing phantom-color and stability-gating checks — the full gate chain is: (1) pre-render filter (formatters.ts applies L1/L2 only), (2) hex check (checkPhantomColors — no fabricated colors), (3) format check (lowercase 6-digit, numeric weights), (4) citation check (every prose claim grounded), (5) section completeness (three-tier required/conditional/optional).

How TIER-1 fixes compose: focus-check (hex format) remains as post-generation gate for class-(c) sections. section-cut (completeness) is updated to three-tier: class-(a) ALWAYS present (formatters guarantee), class-(b) REQUIRED when data exists, class-(c) OPTIONAL with [SKIPPED] marker when insufficient data. AP-29 (stability gating) is wired into formatters.ts as a pre-render filter — only L1+L2 tokens enter class-(a) tables, and validate.ts checks that no L4 colors appear anywhere (covering both auto-rendered and AI-written sections).

Migration path: Phase A (minimal viable, ~20% effort for ~60% gain) = formatters.ts for §2+§3 only + citation gating for §1+§7. Phase B (full architecture) = all class-(a) formatters + DTCG-typed tokens.json + complete citation gating + tokens.css emission + structured section summaries.

focus: Design-system-vs-content boundary detection (cross-page prevalence gate, filter-before-cluster)
answeredQuestions: classifyColorStability uses 5 signals (page coverage, usage dimensions, CSS vars, frequency, achromaticity) but has no hard prevalence gate — frequency bonus can override single-page penalty, leaking content as system

Cross-page prevalence IS a stronger primary gate than OKLCH-frequency score: it's binary (present on N pages or not), can't be gamed by high local frequency, and directly answers the design-token question
Boundary detection SHOULD run before clustering: Superposition-style pre-filtering prevents content colors from contaminating system clusters via deltaE merge, preserving provenance
Hybrid pipeline: prevalence-gate (pages>=2 OR freq>=20 across 2+ dimensions) → OKLCH deltaE<3 cluster → stability classification. Each stage narrows the signal for the next

focus: Composite/semantic tokens reduce re-assembly fabrication (typography/shadow/border/transition tokens + aliasing)
answeredQuestions: DESIGN.md sections requiring primitive re-assembly (fabrication points): §3 Typography Hierarchy (role names invented, though TypographyLevel already composites values), §4 Component Stylings (shadow+typography+border+transition re-assembled from flat Record<string,string> — highest blast-radius), §6 Depth & Elevation (shadow decomposed from flat string for classification), §6.5 Motion (transition parsed from flat string despite TransitionInfo composite existing), §2 Color Palette (color names + personality sentences invented from hex values)

Composite tokens enable direct transcription for: TypographyLevel→§3 (already composite, needs role aliasing), ComponentVariantStyle→§4 (decompose flat record into shadow+border+typography+transition sub-composites), ShadowToken(decomposed)→§6 (deterministic box-shadow rendering + elevation classification), TransitionInfo→§6.5 (already composite, needs threading into ComponentVariant), ColorRole→§2 (provenance-traced naming via CSS var aliasing)

Typed aliasing kills evocative-naming fabrication: yes. An alias {role: 'color.brand.primary', token: ColorToken, sourceVariable: '--color-brand'} replaces 'Stripe Purple' (invented) with a provenance-traced reference. The writer transcribes the role + hex from extraction data instead of inventing a name. The 'personality sentence' remains a fabrication gap (requires design judgment) but the name becomes data-derived.

Schema changes: types.ts needs (1) ComponentVariantStyle decomposed composite (P0), (2) TypographyRole alias (P0), (3) ShadowToken decomposed (P1), (4) ColorRole alias (P1), (5) BorderToken composite (P2), (6) Thread TransitionInfo into ComponentVariant (P2). Extraction changes: DOM collector must decompose box-shadow and border into sub-fields; CSS analyzer must link TransitionInfo to ComponentVariant; post-processing must derive role names from CSS variable names or tag-frequency heuristics.

focus: Cross-track integrated hardening plan (unified backlog, dependencies, minimal-viable vs full-architecture paths)
answeredQuestions: Cross-track deduplication: focus-consistent (A+B+D+V), section-data-driven (A+C+D+V), named-principle (A+C+D+V), source-sentinels (A+C+D+V), interaction-pipeline (B+V), deterministic-renderer (D+X), AP-29+NEVER-QUALITATIVE (C), motion-fallback (A+C), cardinal-rules (A+C), coverage-election (D+V), DTCG (A+D+X) — identified and merged.

Dependencies and sequencing: Extraction fixes (B#1-B#3) MUST precede deterministic rendering (X#1/X#2) as they determine token accuracy. Coverage election (D#2) runs alongside deltaE fix (B#3) in cluster.ts. Section coverage map (B#4) enables data-driven section requirements (A#3). AP-29 + ABSENT-stamp precedes section demotion. Focus check + captured sentinel proves conditional-on-evidence concept before named-principle gating. Deterministic renderer depends on stable extraction output → all B-track fixes first.

Conflicts: (1) 'cut sections/less prose' vs 'narrative value' resolved by evidence-gating not removal (V#3). (2) DTCG migration cost vs anti-fabrication benefit resolved by phased parallel output (V#4). (3) Style-guide §10 deletion test vs format-spec mandatory philosophy — resolved by amendment accepting evidence-backed claims in philosophy sections (C#8).

Minimal-Viable Hardening (~335 LOC, low risk): focus-indicator fix + interaction ON + extractA11yAsync wiring + deltaE threshold fix + coverage election pre-gate (kills hallucination class 1). AP-29 + ABSENT-stamp + NEVER-QUALITATIVE + named-principle conditional + section requirements data-driven + motion OBSERVED/RECOMMENDED + comparative framing removal + section coverage map + validator prose checks (kills hallucination class 2).

Full Architecture (~1200+ LOC, higher risk): everything in Minimal-Viable + deterministic renderer formatters.ts (all class-a sections) + citation gating + DTCG parallel output + tokens.css emission + per-token confidence surfacing + deterministic role assignment + occurrence-band pre-clustering + CSS var resolution + semantic component tagging + all P2 enhancements.

Single highest-leverage change: section requirements data-driven (A#3/C#7/D#1/V#5) — ~40 LOC in validate.ts + format spec that eliminates the structural fabrication pressure for data-poor sections across all 5 gold-standard-absent sections. Every track independently identified this as the top priority.

focus: Final external sweep (re-extract drift score, incremental merge, survey-completeness)
answeredQuestions: proof.ts drift detection IS a freshness score — coverage % + timestamp → DESIGN.md header stamp (FRESH/STALE) is adoptable at LOW cost

--merge-with mergeTokenSets correctly preserves stable tokens (deltaE<3 dedup, frequency accumulation) but lacks per-token provenance tracking (extractionCount, lastSeenDate) and silently overwrites non-token fields

External survey is COMPLETE: 22+ tools given verdicts across iterations 001-034 (design-extract, CSS Stats, Tokens Studio, Style Dictionary, Specify, Supernova, Zeroheight, Knapsack, Anima, Diez, Backlight, Locofy, Builder.io, Polypane, Contrast, Stark, axe-core, MiroMiro, project-wallace, get-custom-properties-style, font-converters, Tokens Studio two-way sync). iteration-029 captures 23 ranked items; this iteration adds 3 NEW findings (freshness stamp, merge provenance, multi-brand theming) and confirms 12 prior-verdict tools as already-captured.

No new borrowable ideas from un-surveyed tools remain — all tools mentioned in the prompt (Knapsack, Zeroheight, Anima, font-converters) were either already captured in iteration-029 or yield DEFER verdicts

focus: Minimum-viable typed-token schema (section->DTCG $type map, additive typed layer, deterministic-render bridge)
answeredQuestions: Which DESIGN.md sections map to which DTCG $types: §2→color, §3→typography composite, §5→dimension, §6→shadow composite, §6.5→duration+cubicBezier+transition composite, §12→dimension+strokeStyle+number, §4 components→border+transition composites. §1/7/8/10/13 have no DTCG type (qualitative prose).

Minimum-viable schema: add $type (DTCG enum union) + id (stable content-hash) to each existing token interface. No interface shape changes. A DTCGView projection type provides the typed alias layer. This is ~80 LOC, additive only.

Deterministically renderable sections with typing: §3 Typography Hierarchy (from TypographyLevel[] + roleLabel), §6 Depth table (from ShadowToken[] + parsed shadow parts + shadowLevelLabel), §6.5 Motion tables (from MotionSystem entries with $type projection), §5 Border Radius Scale (from RadiusToken[] + $type dimension). §2 Color Palette is already near-deterministic (need id for citation).

Additive path confirmed: $type + id fields are the ONLY extraction changes. The projection layer (DTCGView) is a pure function over existing data. Full DTCG replacement with alias resolution and composite normalization is deferred, zero extraction rewrite.

focus: Verify solution architecture + 335-LOC minimal path (SOUND/CAVEAT/OVER-CLAIMED)
answeredQuestions: Is the ~335 LOC claim realistic? NO — summed individual estimates total ~550 LOC. Realistic scope for the listed changes is ~430 LOC (±50). Re-baseline needed.

Does minimal-viable kill both hallucination classes? MOSTLY — class-1 (value fabrication) has clean fixes for the dominant cases but edge cases remain (interaction-data-propagation fixes at ~120 LOC are deferred to P1). class-2 (prose fabrication) is killed by REMOVING the mandates that require fabrication (section data-driven, comparative-framing removal, ABSENT-stamp, named-principle conditional), not by post-generation enforcement — the citation-gating that would actively verify prose claims is deferred to full architecture.

Is interaction-ON-by-default safe? YES — PAGE_TIMEOUT=30s hard-caps per-page cost. The plan's '~5min per page' estimate is INACCURATE. Actual worst case: 30s/page × 8 pages = 4min total. The --fast-no-interaction opt-out provides an adequate escape hatch. No additional budget cap needed.

Does deterministic rendering have hidden complexity? YES — named-principle classification (design_md_format.md:544) requires AI judgment about token patterns that cannot be fully automated. Citation gating proves token grounding, not claim truth. The [INFERRED] label system is honest about this limitation.

focus: Ordered implementation backlog (phased tasks, file targets, per-change validation, ship order)
answeredQuestions: What is the optimal ship order? Extraction fixes (Phase 1) FIRST — all doc/validator/architecture fixes depend on accurate token data.
What are the MINIMAL-VIABLE task boundaries? T1-T17 (~335 LOC) kills both hallucination classes 1 (value fabrication) and 2 (prose fabrication).
What is the single highest-leverage change? T6 (section-requirements data-driven) — ~45 LOC eliminating structural fabrication pressure across 5 data-poor sections.
What does Phase 4 (doc-as-view) depend on? All Phase 1-3 tasks must be verified working; formatters.ts Phase A (§2+§3 only) can ship independently.

answeredQuestions: D: What do the best external extractors do? ANSWERED — 23 ranked borrow items across iterations 1-34, top 3: data-driven sections (Open Design), deterministic tables (Diez/Supernova), coverage election (design-extract). Survey confirmed COMPLETE for the major design-extraction ecosystem.

A(partial): What mechanical validator catches prose fabrication? PARTIALLY ANSWERED — banished-word check + source sentinels designed but not implemented. Citation gating designed (iter-030) but deferred to full architecture.

B(partial): Which extraction gaps force fabrication? MOSTLY ANSWERED — 14 specific bugs identified with file:line evidence (iter-027). Interaction pipeline, a11yAsync dead code, deltaE threshold confirmed real.

focus: WRITE prompt template audit + definitive deterministic-vs-prose section split
answeredQuestions: Which sections of the 17-section format require deterministic transcription vs. prose inference? (Resolved: the definitive split below supersedes all prior contradictory takes from earlier iterations.)

Does the WRITE-phase prompt template contain fabrication-enabling gaps? (Yes — 12 findings spanning ungoverned prose, missing inference-labels, conflicting existence rules, and a validation gate blind to everything except hex and section count.)

focus: Un-audited extraction modules (icon/framework/dark-mode/crawl) default-fabrication + starvation audit
answeredQuestions: Does icon-detect have the default-fabrication pattern? YES — determineColorMode returns 'mixed' on empty svgColors (line 78)
Does framework-detect return a guessed framework on weak evidence? NO — returns null for each sub-detector when not found; threshold is fixed but reasonable
Does dark-mode-detect claim support without a real palette? YES — supported:true when variableDiff is empty (line 258-265), which is the most impactful fabrication
Is dark-mode §2.5 gating correct? NO — it gates on supported:true, not on variableDiff.length > 0; sites with detection triggers but no actual CSS changes get full dark-mode sections
Which modules are clean? crawl.ts, css-analyzer.ts (no default-fabrication or starvation patterns found)

focus: WRITE-phase construction trace (raw-JSON manual write; prompt-builder/formatters structural fix)
answeredQuestions: How does the WRITE phase actually work? — Confirmed: it is fully manual. There is NO script-based prompt-builder, compose script, or formatter. The agent reads tokens.json + the format spec and composes DESIGN.md by hand. The 'prompt template' at assets/design_md_prompt_template.md is a copy-paste text block with placeholder slots, not a programmatic builder.

Where does fabrication enter? — (a) The model receives raw, unfiltered JSON with L1-L4 tokens mixed together; it must filter by stability class manually. (b) Empty conditional sections (dark mode, icons, motion) are expressed as boolean/null fields, not explicit ABSENT signals. (c) All 17 section values must be manually found in nested JSON arrays. (d) The 'cardinal rules' are prose, not structural constraints — the model can ignore them. (e) Validation is post-hoc (validate.ts), not upfront.

Should there be a prompt-builder script? — Yes. It is the single highest-leverage structural fix. It composes with iter-30 (doc-as-view) and is the concrete instantiation of iter-38's formatters.ts: per-section deterministic rendering functions that transform typed tokens into pre-rendered value-tables, with explicit DATA_AVAILABILITY markers for conditional sections.

focus: Round-3 verification of high-risk Track-B extraction fixes (SOUND/CAVEAT/REFUTED + regression guards)
answeredQuestions: Q1 findInteraction: key mismatch confirmed at cluster.ts:1294 vs 1288 — third slot uses el.tag instead of componentType; linear-scan fallback direction is inverted (el.className.includes(cap.classes) fails when representative has fewer classes)
Q2 interaction-ON-by-default: 30s/page cap is real and enforced (line 740 checkpoint between elements, not after all 50); worst-case adds 4min to 8-page extraction; --fast already suppresses

Q3 deltaE threshold: raising from 3→8 merges brand scale variants (deltaE≈8.1 for #3B82F6→#60A5FA) but risks merging functionally different colors without a usage-dimension gate; deltaE 5 is safer without the gate
Q4 prevalence gate: conservative AND gate (pages<2 AND freq<10 AND dims≤1) catches #646464 while passing brand colors on 2+ pages OR freq≥10 OR 2+ dims; needs CSS-var bypass exception
Q5 extractA11yAsync: async fields are real (tabOrder/lang/skipLink/altTextCoverage); wiring into per-page loop is additive; sync path untouched; dead-code clusterTokens a11y should be removed
